title: Create branch feature/preset-questions
description: Create branch 'feature/preset-questions' from current HEAD (feature/answer-guardrails). Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine.

title: Migration + model + schemas for question mode
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ each file before editing.

1. CREATE backend/alembic/versions/002_add_question_mode.py — Alembic migration:
   - Read 001_initial_schema.py for the pattern/style.
   - revision = '002', down_revision = '001'
   - upgrade(): 3 ALTER TABLE statements:
     ALTER TABLE surveys ADD COLUMN question_mode TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'dynamic';
     ALTER TABLE surveys ADD COLUMN preset_questions TEXT NULL;
     ALTER TABLE surveys ADD COLUMN preset_generated_at TEXT NULL;
   - downgrade(): 3 ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN statements (use batch_alter_table for SQLite).

2. EDIT backend/app/models/survey.py — Add to Survey class:
   question_mode = Column(Text, nullable=False, default='dynamic')
   preset_questions = Column(Text, nullable=True)
   preset_generated_at = Column(Text, nullable=True)

3. EDIT backend/app/schemas/survey.py — READ first, then:
   - Add import: from typing import Literal, Optional (if not present)
   - Add new schema class PresetQuestion(BaseModel): question_number: int, question_id: str, text: str
   - In CreateSurveyRequest: add question_mode: Literal['preset', 'dynamic'] = 'dynamic'
   - In UpdateSurveyRequest: add question_mode: Optional[Literal['preset', 'dynamic']] = None
   - In SurveyResponse: add question_mode: str, preset_questions: Optional[list[PresetQuestion]] = None, preset_generated_at: Optional[str] = None

title: Generator agent + prompts for preset generation
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ each file first.

1. EDIT backend/app/agents/prompts.py — Add new function build_preset_generation_prompt():
   - Parameters: survey (object with context, goal, constraints), generated_so_far (list of dicts with question_number and text), question_number (int), max_questions (int)
   - Build a prompt similar to build_generator_prompt() but:
     a. Include survey context, goal, constraints
     b. Instead of real Q&A history, show previously generated questions: 'Q1: {text}\nQ2: {text}\n...'
     c. Add instruction: 'You are generating a fixed question set for this survey. There are no real participant answers yet. Generate question {question_number} of {max_questions}. Ensure this question covers a different facet of the survey goal than previous questions. Focus on comprehensive coverage across all questions.'
     d. Include the SECURITY block (since this is still an LLM prompt)

2. EDIT backend/app/agents/generator_agent.py — Add new async function generate_preset_question_set(survey, count: int) -> list[dict]:
   - Import uuid4 from uuid
   - Import build_preset_generation_prompt from prompts
   - Loop count times (i from 1 to count):
     a. Build prompt using build_preset_generation_prompt(survey, generated_so_far, i, count)
     b. Call the existing LLM via the agent (reuse the same model/agent pattern as generate_question but with the preset prompt)
     c. Run the question through the validator (call validate_with_llm and check_compound_question, check_leading_question) for quality
     d. Retry up to 3 times if validation fails (same pattern as generate_question)
     e. Append {question_number: i, question_id: str(uuid4()), text: question_text} to generated_so_far
   - Return generated_so_far
   - Handle errors gracefully — if generation fails for a question after retries, use a sensible fallback or raise

title: Survey service preset generation logic
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ backend/app/services/survey_service.py first.

1. Add imports: import json, from datetime import datetime, from app.agents.generator_agent import generate_preset_question_set

2. Update create_survey() — Accept question_mode parameter. Pass it to the Survey() constructor. Do NOT auto-generate questions here (admin triggers separately).

3. Add new async function generate_preset_questions(survey_id: str, db: AsyncSession) -> list[dict]:
   - Load survey by ID. If not found, raise ValueError.
   - Validate survey.question_mode == 'preset'. If not, raise ValueError('Survey is not in preset mode').
   - Call: questions = await generate_preset_question_set(survey, survey.max_questions)
   - Store: survey.preset_questions = json.dumps(questions)
   - Store: survey.preset_generated_at = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
   - Commit.
   - Return questions.

4. Add new async function update_preset_questions(survey_id: str, questions: list[dict], db: AsyncSession) -> None:
   - Load survey. Validate mode is preset.
   - survey.preset_questions = json.dumps(questions)
   - survey.preset_generated_at = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
   - Commit.

title: Question service mode branching
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ backend/app/services/question_service.py first.

1. Add import: import json

2. In generate_next_question() function, add a branch at the TOP (after loading survey and session, before any LLM calls):

   if survey.question_mode == 'preset':
       return await _get_next_preset_question(survey, session, db)
   # else: existing dynamic logic continues

3. Add new async helper function _get_next_preset_question(survey, session, db) -> str:
   - If survey.preset_questions is None or survey.preset_questions == '': raise ValueError('Preset questions not yet generated for this survey.')
   - Parse: questions = json.loads(survey.preset_questions)
   - current_index = session.question_count  # 0-based index
   - If current_index >= len(questions): mark session as completed, return completion message (same pattern as max_questions check)
   - Return questions[current_index]['text']

4. IMPORTANT: For preset mode, do NOT call estimate_goal_coverage(). Do NOT call the validator. Just return the preset question directly. The goal coverage and validation were already done at generation time.

title: Admin API new endpoints + serialization
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ backend/app/api/admin.py first.

1. Update _survey_to_response() helper — Add to the response dict:
   - 'question_mode': survey.question_mode
   - 'preset_questions': json.loads(survey.preset_questions) if survey.preset_questions else None
   - 'preset_generated_at': survey.preset_generated_at
   Add import json at top if not present.

2. Update create_survey endpoint — Read the question_mode from request body and pass to survey_service.create_survey(). The CreateSurveyRequest schema already has the field from Task 2.

3. Add NEW endpoint POST /admin/surveys/{survey_id}/generate-questions:
   - Import generate_preset_questions from survey_service
   - Call: questions = await survey_service.generate_preset_questions(survey_id, db)
   - Return: {'questions': questions, 'generated_at': datetime.utcnow().isoformat()}
   - Handle ValueError (wrong mode, not found) with HTTPException 400/404
   - This endpoint may take 30-60s for many questions. That's OK for v1.

4. Add NEW endpoint PUT /admin/surveys/{survey_id}/preset-questions:
   - Accept body: {'questions': list of PresetQuestion}
   - Import from schemas: PresetQuestion
   - Call: await survey_service.update_preset_questions(survey_id, [q.model_dump() for q in request.questions], db)
   - Return: {'status': 'updated'}

5. Make sure the update_survey endpoint also handles question_mode changes.

title: Backend tests for preset questions
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. Create NEW file backend/tests/test_preset_questions.py.

Tests to write:

1. test_create_survey_default_mode: Create survey without question_mode -> defaults to 'dynamic'
2. test_create_survey_preset_mode: Create survey with question_mode='preset' -> stored correctly
3. test_create_survey_invalid_mode: Create survey with question_mode='invalid' -> 422 validation error
4. test_generate_preset_questions_endpoint: POST to generate-questions endpoint with a preset survey. Mock the LLM calls (like existing tests do). Verify questions are returned and stored.
5. test_generate_preset_questions_wrong_mode: POST to generate-questions for a dynamic survey -> 400 error
6. test_preset_question_serving: Create a preset survey with stored questions. Create a session. Verify first question matches preset_questions[0]. Submit answer. Verify second question matches preset_questions[1].
7. test_preset_mode_skips_coverage: Verify that preset mode does NOT call estimate_goal_coverage or the LLM validator.
8. test_update_preset_questions: PUT to update questions endpoint. Verify questions are updated.
9. test_preset_completion: Submit answers for all preset questions. Verify session completes.
10. test_preset_no_questions_generated: Try to start session on preset survey with no questions -> appropriate error.
11. test_survey_response_includes_mode: GET survey includes question_mode field.
12. test_build_preset_generation_prompt: Import and call build_preset_generation_prompt, verify it returns sensible prompt text.

Run FULL test suite: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -v. ALL tests must pass (existing 107 + new). Fix any broken tests.

title: Backend code review
description:
Review ALL modified and new backend files. Check:
1. Migration is correct (proper ALTER TABLE syntax for SQLite, batch operations in downgrade).
2. Survey model has all 3 new columns with correct types/defaults.
3. Schemas validate question_mode correctly (Literal type, not free string).
4. generate_preset_question_set() properly builds synthetic history and validates each question.
5. build_preset_generation_prompt() produces coherent prompts that encourage question diversity.
6. question_service branching is clean — preset path does NO LLM calls, dynamic path is unchanged.
7. Admin API endpoints have proper error handling (404, 400 for wrong mode).
8. _survey_to_response() correctly serializes preset_questions from JSON.
9. generate_preset_questions in survey_service commits after storing questions.
10. No regressions in existing dynamic flow.
11. Preset questions JSON format is consistent across generation, storage, and serving.

title: Frontend implementation
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ each file before editing.

1. EDIT frontend/src/types/survey.ts:
   - Add PresetQuestion interface: { question_number: number; question_id: string; text: string; }
   - Add to CreateSurveyRequest: question_mode?: 'preset' | 'dynamic'
   - Add to SurveyResponse (or equivalent response type): question_mode: 'preset' | 'dynamic', preset_questions?: PresetQuestion[], preset_generated_at?: string

2. EDIT frontend/src/services/api.ts:
   - Add: generatePresetQuestions(surveyId: string) — POST to /api/v1/admin/surveys/{surveyId}/generate-questions
   - Add: updatePresetQuestions(surveyId: string, questions: PresetQuestion[]) — PUT to /api/v1/admin/surveys/{surveyId}/preset-questions

3. EDIT frontend/src/components/SurveyForm.tsx:
   - Add question_mode to form state (default 'dynamic')
   - Add radio group after existing form fields:
     Label: 'Question Mode'
     Options: 'Dynamic — AI generates unique questions per participant' (value: 'dynamic'), 'Preset — AI generates fixed questions once, same for all participants' (value: 'preset')
   - Style with Tailwind to match existing form style
   - Include question_mode in the onSubmit payload

4. EDIT frontend/src/pages/SurveyDetail.tsx:
   - Show question_mode badge next to survey title: blue pill 'Dynamic' or purple pill 'Preset'
   - If preset mode:
     a. If preset_questions exists: show numbered list of questions in a card. Each item shows 'Q{n}: {text}'.
     b. Show 'Generated at: {preset_generated_at}' timestamp
     c. Show 'Regenerate Questions' button (with confirm dialog)
     d. If NO preset_questions: show alert banner 'Questions not generated yet' + 'Generate Questions' button
     e. Generate button onClick: call api.generatePresetQuestions(), show loading spinner, refresh data on success
   - If dynamic mode: show existing content as-is (no changes)

5. EDIT frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.tsx:
   - On each survey card, add a small badge showing the question mode ('Dynamic' / 'Preset')
   - Use subtle styling (small pill, muted colors) to not overwhelm the card

title: Frontend tests
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. Verify frontend changes:
1. SurveyForm renders mode selector radio buttons.
2. Default mode is 'dynamic'.
3. Switching to 'preset' updates form state.
4. SurveyDetail shows correct badge for each mode.
5. Generate Questions button appears only for preset surveys without questions.
6. Regenerate button appears for preset surveys with existing questions.
7. AdminDashboard shows mode badge on cards.
8. TypeScript types compile without errors.
9. Run: cd frontend && npm run build to verify no build errors.

title: Frontend code review
description:
Review all frontend changes:
1. Types match the API contract (PresetQuestion, question_mode on request/response).
2. API functions use correct endpoints and HTTP methods.
3. SurveyForm mode selector has good UX (clear labels, info text, accessible).
4. SurveyDetail preset section handles all states: no questions, loading, questions present, error.
5. Regenerate has confirmation dialog to prevent accidental overwrite.
6. Loading states during generation (30-60s) are clear to the user.
7. AdminDashboard badges are unobtrusive.
8. Tailwind classes are consistent with existing design.
9. No TypeScript errors or warnings.

title: Architecture review — cross-layer
description:
Review the entire feature across all layers:
1. DB schema → Model → Schema → API → Frontend type chain is consistent for question_mode, preset_questions, preset_generated_at.
2. API contract is clean — new endpoints follow RESTful patterns, response shapes are consistent.
3. Preset generation reuses the validator pipeline correctly (no skipped quality checks at generation time).
4. Preset serving correctly SKIPS LLM calls and coverage checks (no wasted resources).
5. Dynamic mode is COMPLETELY UNCHANGED — no regressions possible.
6. Error states are handled: no questions generated, wrong mode, generation failure.
7. Frontend handles long generation times gracefully.
8. Answer guardrails still run for preset mode (answers still need validation).
9. No security concerns with storing/serving preset questions.
10. Mode switching (dynamic↔preset) is safe with active sessions.

title: Push feature/preset-questions branch
description: Commit all changes and push to 'feature/preset-questions' branch. Commit message: 'feat: add preset vs dynamic question mode — admin can generate fixed question sets or use dynamic AI generation'. Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine.
